page: Amazon Web Services m6in.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, and is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1611.0 and a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1891.98. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 3433.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3862.55. Memory bandwidth is also in the lower tier, measuring 27.84 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 22.89 GB/sec for large block write operations.

The instance demonstrates moderate efficiency in specific workloads, achieving average ratings in static web serving throughput at 6.32 GB/sec, single-threaded gzip compression at 28.24 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 9.98 GB/sec. Conversely, database operations and Redis SET performance are weak, with Redis registering 3.91 million operations per second. This hardware profile is best suited for general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight single-threaded tasks rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized database applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C x86_64 processor running at 3.5 GHz, offering 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1611.0, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring balanced compute-to-memory ratios without heavy parallel processing demands. This instance is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.5 GHz CPU, optimized for network and EBS workloads under the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.xlarge is a general-purpose Nitro instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A general-purpose, network-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Optimized for network and EBS performance with 6 Gbps baseline bandwidth
Average single-core performance suitable for single-threaded processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and basic utility workloads
